reforms.description: Germany is boosting public investment and incentives for private investment through a recovery package that sets aside EUR 50 billion, aimed at addressing the public investment backlog and enhancing infrastructure capacity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public and private investment in infrastructure is expected to enhance productivity and support economic recovery.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is actively increasing public investment as part of its recovery efforts.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in public investment strategy, addressing long-standing infrastructure deficits and aiming to stimulate economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government is boosting public investment and incentives for private investment through setting aside EUR 50 billion as part of its recovery package.

reforms.description: The COVID-19 crisis has highlighted the need for Germany to accelerate its digital transformation, with a focus on enhancing fixed and mobile communication network coverage and quality.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Accelerating digital transformation is expected to improve productivity and economic resilience, particularly in response to the challenges posed by the COVID-19 crisis.
reforms.status_evidence: The need for digital transformation has been recognized and prioritized in response to the COVID-19 crisis.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a critical shift towards enhancing digital infrastructure and capabilities, which are essential for future economic growth and resilience.
reforms.source_quote: Digital transformation should be accelerated through enhancing fixed and mobile communication network coverage and quality.

reforms.description: The German government plans to increase investments in digital transformation, education, health, and green energy as part of a recovery package, which includes direct subsidies for research on digitalisation and energy transition technologies.
reforms.component_name: Investment in Digital Transformation and Green Energy
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public investment in R&D and digitalisation is expected to enhance productivity and innovation capacity, driving long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to bring forward public investment in various sectors as part of the recovery package.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in public investment strategy aimed at addressing long-term challenges and fostering innovation across multiple sectors.
reforms.source_quote: About EUR 50 billion seeks to respond to long-term challenges through public and private investment in the domains of digital transformation, education, health and green energy.

reforms.description: The German government is increasing R&D tax incentives to stimulate private investment in research and development, particularly in digitalisation and energy transition.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing R&D tax incentives is expected to encourage firms to invest more in innovation, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The recovery package includes measures to support R&D and accelerate digital transformation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a major policy change aimed at boosting private sector investment in R&D, which is crucial for long-term economic resilience and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Increased R&D tax incentives and direct subsidies for research on digitalisation, e-mobility and energy transition including hydrogen technologies.

reforms.description: The German government is developing an action plan to strengthen financial regulation, which includes giving the Federal Financial Supervisory Authority (BaFin) new authority and requiring companies to change their auditors more frequently.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Strengthening financial regulation can enhance market confidence and stability, which is essential for fostering innovation and investment.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is developing an action plan to strengthen financial regulation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses significant weaknesses in financial oversight, which can have broad implications for the stability of the financial system and its ability to support innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government is developing an 'action plan' to strengthen financial regulation, give BaFin new authority and force companies to change their auditors more frequently.

reforms.description: Germany plans to increase the eligibility cap for R&D tax incentives to EUR 10 million, aiming to enhance support for innovative firms and stimulate private investment in research and development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By raising the cap, the reform is expected to incentivize more firms to invest in R&D, thereby enhancing innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans to increase the eligibility cap for R&D tax incentives.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment to existing tax incentives, potentially increasing R&D investment among firms.
reforms.source_quote: Increase the eligibility cap for R&D tax incentives to EUR 10 million.

reforms.description: The Climate Action Programme 2030 expanded federal funding programmes for charging station infrastructure on a large scale and additional funding was announced in the June 2020 recovery package.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in charging infrastructure supports the transition to electric vehicles, which can enhance productivity and reduce emissions.
reforms.status_evidence: Funding for charging station infrastructure was expanded as part of the recovery package.
reforms.importance_rationale: The expansion of funding for charging infrastructure is significant for promoting electric vehicle adoption and reducing emissions, contributing to broader climate goals.
reforms.source_quote: The Climate Action Programme 2030 expanded federal funding programmes for charging station infrastructure on a large scale.

reforms.description: The federal government has announced EUR 40 billion in support for coal mining regions until 2038, focusing on supporting infrastructure, innovation, and job markets.
reforms.component_name: Support for Innovation and Job Markets
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This support aims to foster innovation and job creation in regions transitioning away from coal, which can enhance local economies and reduce inequalities.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced substantial financial support for coal mining regions.
reforms.importance_rationale: The EUR 40 billion support package is significant for addressing the economic impacts of transitioning from coal, promoting innovation and job creation in affected regions.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government has announced EUR 40 billion in support for coal mining regions until 2038, focusing on supporting infrastructure, innovation, and job markets.

reforms.description: The Act on Opportunities to Gain Qualifications, effective since 2019, has expanded training opportunities for individuals affected by structural change, allowing employers to receive wage subsidies for skill development programs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances the skill set of the workforce, thereby increasing productivity and adaptability in a changing job market.
reforms.status_evidence: The Act has been in effect since 2019, expanding training opportunities.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly enhances training opportunities for a broad segment of the workforce, addressing skill shortages and promoting lifelong learning.
reforms.source_quote: The Act on Opportunities to Gain Qualifications, in effect since 2019, has expanded training opportunities for individuals independent of age, qualification and firm size.

reforms.description: The 'Arbeit-von-morgen-Gesetz' implemented in 2020 aims to strengthen training in response to structural changes due to digitalisation and climate change, increasing support rates for firms providing training.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increases the skill level of the workforce, which is essential for adapting to technological advancements and ensuring economic resilience.
reforms.status_evidence: The law was implemented in 2020 to strengthen training further in view of ongoing structural change.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform addresses critical skill gaps in the workforce, promoting adaptability to future job market demands.
reforms.source_quote: In 2020, the 'Arbeit-von-morgen-Gesetz' was implemented to strengthen training further in view of ongoing structural change related to digitalisation and climate change.

reforms.description: Germany is developing a comprehensive national digital transformation strategy to enhance policy coherence and coordination across various digital-related initiatives, aiming to maximize the benefits of digital transformation for productivity and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: A coherent digital transformation strategy is expected to enhance productivity and innovation by improving the adoption of ICT tools and fostering a supportive environment for digital initiatives.
reforms.status_evidence: The implementation roadmap Digitalisierung Gestalten presents an important step towards a comprehensive national digital transformation strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This strategy represents a structural change in how Germany approaches digital transformation, aiming to unify various initiatives under a coherent framework, which is crucial for enhancing overall productivity and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The implementation roadmap Digitalisierung Gestalten presents an important step towards a comprehensive national digital transformation strategy.

reforms.description: The German government has set an ambitious goal for nationwide gigabit Internet coverage by 2025, supported by a state aid programme that allocates around EUR 11 billion for broadband deployment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving broadband infrastructure is essential for enhancing digital connectivity, which can drive productivity and innovation across various sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has allocated significant funds for broadband deployment as part of its coalition agreement.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a structural change in Germany's digital infrastructure policy, aiming to significantly enhance connectivity and support economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government has put in place a number of public broadband subsidies... to achieve this goal.

reforms.description: Germany's national mobile strategy aims to achieve 99% LTE coverage for all households by the end of 2020 and to enhance 5G infrastructure with an additional EUR 5 billion investment post-COVID-19.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving mobile infrastructure is essential for enhancing connectivity, which supports digital transformation and innovation across sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: Contracts signed with mobile network operators aim for ambitious coverage targets.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant investment in national infrastructure that is crucial for future economic growth and competitiveness in the digital age.
reforms.source_quote: The German mobile strategy aims at closing gaps in the LTE network and making Germany a leading country in fifth generation cellular networks (5G).

reforms.description: The 2019 auction of spectrum in the 2 GHz and 3.6 GHz bands is designed to facilitate 5G deployment and improve mobile network competition in Germany.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The auction promotes competition among mobile operators, which can lead to better services and innovation in mobile technology.
reforms.status_evidence: The auction allowed for the entrance of a new player with 1&1 Drillisch, which has the potential to significantly spur competition.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the auction is a specific measure within a broader strategy to enhance mobile network competition and infrastructure.
reforms.source_quote: The 2019 auction of spectrum in the 2 Gigahertz (GHz) and 3.6 GHz bands paved the way for 5G deployment in the country.

reforms.description: Germany's government is developing a Data Strategy aimed at improving data sharing, promoting responsible data usage, and enhancing data competencies to boost innovation across sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing data sharing and usage is expected to foster innovation and improve productivity across various sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is developing a Data Strategy covering four main areas.
reforms.importance_rationale: The strategy addresses critical areas for enhancing data governance and usage, which are essential for fostering innovation in the digital economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government is also developing a Data Strategy, which has been announced as covering four main areas: 1) improving data sharing and securing access to data, 2) promoting responsible data usage and increasing the potential for innovation.

reforms.description: The GAIA-X project aims to create a federated data infrastructure for Europe, enhancing data sharing and innovation to strengthen digital sovereignty.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering a robust data infrastructure, GAIA-X is expected to enhance innovation capabilities and competitiveness in the digital market.
reforms.status_evidence: GAIA-X is an ambitious project to create a federated and trustworthy data infrastructure for Europe.
reforms.importance_rationale: The project represents a significant initiative to enhance data infrastructure, which is crucial for innovation and competitiveness in the digital economy.
reforms.source_quote: GAIA-X is an ambitious project to create a federated and trustworthy data infrastructure for Europe in a strengthened European Digital Single Market.

reforms.description: Germany introduced R&D tax incentives in early 2020, subsidising 25% of maximum EUR 2 million R&D expenditures per year, with an increased cap of EUR 4 million per firm until the end of 2025 as part of the COVID-19 recovery package.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax incentives are designed to stimulate R&D investment among firms, particularly benefiting SMEs, which can enhance innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The R&D tax incentives were introduced and are currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the support for business R&D, aiming to enhance innovation capacity across the economy.
reforms.source_quote: Germany introduced R&D tax incentives in early 2020, subsidising 25% of maximum EUR 2 million R&D expenditures per year.

reforms.description: The government’s mFund supports investment in data-driven innovation, research projects, SMEs, and start-ups in mobility, with EUR 200 million allocated for 2016-2020 and EUR 250 million starting in 2021.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing financial support to SMEs and start-ups, the mFund aims to foster innovation and competitiveness in the mobility sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The mFund has been allocated funding and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The mFund represents a significant investment in the startup ecosystem for mobility, enhancing the capacity for innovation in this sector.
reforms.source_quote: The government’s mFund supports investment in data-driven innovation, research projects, SMEs and start-ups in mobility.

reforms.description: The German government launched the Digital Jetzt (Digital Now) programme, which subsidizes investments in ICTs, including software and related training for SMEs with 3-499 employees.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By subsidizing ICT investments, the programme enhances SMEs' capabilities, fostering innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme was launched to support SMEs in their digital transformation efforts.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant investment in digital capabilities for SMEs, addressing a critical barrier to their growth and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The recently launched Digital Jetzt (Digital Now) programme subsidises investments in ICTs, including software and related training in firms with 3-499 employees.

reforms.description: The Mittelstand-Digital strategy was introduced to raise awareness and provide guidance on digital transformation for SMEs, including the establishment of local Kompetenzzentren.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing awareness and providing expertise, the strategy supports SMEs in adopting digital technologies, which can lead to increased productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy was launched to support SMEs in their digital transformation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The strategy provides a structured approach to digital transformation for SMEs, which is crucial for enhancing their competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The government’s 2019 Mittelstand-Digital strategy raises awareness and provides guidance on digital transformation for SMEs.

reforms.description: Germany is considering strengthening the role of KfW Capital to become more active in growth financing and is examining approaches to make investment conditions more attractive to institutional investors, including the establishment of an additional EUR 10 billion start-up fund for next generation technology.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing growth financing for start-ups can stimulate innovation and entrepreneurship, leading to increased economic activity and job creation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is considering strengthening the role of KfW Capital and establishing a new start-up fund.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant investment in the start-up ecosystem, aiming to enhance the availability of venture capital and support innovative firms, which is crucial for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government is considering strengthening the role of KfW Capital to become more active in growth financing and is examining approaches to make investment conditions more attractive to institutional investors.

reforms.description: Germany's Online Access Act mandates public administrations to offer their services digitally by 2022, aiming to improve efficiency and user-friendliness in public service delivery.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Digitalizing public services can reduce administrative burdens for businesses, leading to increased efficiency and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The Online Access Act mandates public administrations to offer their services digitally by 2022.
reforms.importance_rationale: This act represents a structural change in how public services are delivered, aiming to enhance efficiency and accessibility, which is vital for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The Online Access Act mandates public administrations to offer their services digitally by 2022.

reforms.description: The federal government has allocated about EUR 6.5 billion to improve digital infrastructure in schools through the DigitalPakt Schule initiative, aiming to provide all schools with broadband connections and suitable devices for teachers and students.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving digital infrastructure in schools enhances educational outcomes, which can lead to a more skilled workforce and increased productivity in the long run.
reforms.status_evidence: The federal government has allocated about EUR 6.5 billion to improve digital infrastructure.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant investment in educational infrastructure, which is crucial for adapting to the digital age and improving overall educational quality.
reforms.source_quote: As part of the DigitalPakt Schule, the federal government has allocated about EUR 6.5 billion to improve digital infrastructure.

reforms.description: The STEM Action Plan, introduced in 2019, aims to raise enthusiasm for STEM education and careers, particularly focusing on girls and women's needs through networking, funding, and communication measures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing the number of STEM graduates, the plan aims to enhance the skilled workforce necessary for innovation and technological advancement.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan was introduced to address the need for more STEM graduates.
reforms.importance_rationale: The plan represents a significant effort to address skills shortages in STEM fields, which are critical for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The STEM Action Plan, introduced in 2019, aims to raise enthusiasm to take up STEM education and careers.

reforms.description: The Work of Tomorrow Act, from 2020, aims to facilitate adult learning through increased financial support and decreased bureaucratic efforts for employers and employees.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing adult education and training, the act aims to improve the skills of the workforce, thereby supporting innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The act was designed to facilitate adult learning through various supportive measures.
reforms.importance_rationale: The act represents a significant initiative to improve adult education, which is essential for adapting to changing labor market demands.
reforms.source_quote: The Work of Tomorrow Act (Arbeit-von-Morgen-Gesetz), from 2020, aims to facilitate adult learning through increased financial support.

reforms.description: Germany is reviewing the cap for R&D tax incentives to make them more applicable to mid-range companies, aiming to improve conditions for firms to invest in knowledge-based capital crucial for data-driven innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing R&D tax incentives, the reform encourages firms to invest in innovation, which can lead to increased productivity and competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is reviewing the cap for R&D tax incentives.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment to existing tax incentives, potentially impacting a broader range of firms and enhancing their capacity to innovate.
reforms.source_quote: Improve conditions for firms to invest in knowledge-based capital, including by reviewing the cap for R&D tax incentives to make them more applicable to mid-range companies.

reforms.description: Germany aims to improve the effectiveness of start-up and growth financing instruments by scaling up later stage funding and enhancing conditions for institutional investors to invest in venture capital.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing financing conditions for startups can stimulate innovation and entrepreneurship, leading to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Improve the effectiveness of start-up and growth financing instruments.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it targets the critical area of financing for startups, which is essential for fostering innovation and economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: Improve the effectiveness of start-up and growth financing instruments, including by avoiding complexity, scaling up later stage funding and improving conditions for institutional investors to invest in venture capital.
